        'float': ctypes.c_float,

        'void': ctypes.c_void_p,
    }

    def __init__(self, name, type_str, size=None, alignment=None):
        self.name = name
        self.type_str = type_str
        self.size = size
        self.alignment = alignment
        self.primitive_type = self.PRIMITIVE_TYPES.get(type_str, None)

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"Type(name='{self.name}', type_str='{self.type_str}', size={self.size}, alignment={self.alignment})"

# Example usage:
if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Create a type object for a 32-bit integer
    int_type = Type('int', 'int', size=4, alignment=4)
    print(int_type)

    # Create a type object for a custom struct
    struct_type = Type('MyStruct', 'struct', size=16, alignment=8)
    print(struct_type)

def get_events():
    events = []
    for i in range(1, 10):
        date = datetime.now().strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
        title = f"Event {i}"
        text = f"This is the text for event {i}."
        url = f"https://example.com/event{i}"
        image = f"https://example.com/image{i}.jpg"
        tags = ["tag1", "tag2", "tag3"]
        event = {
            "date": date,
            "rev": i, 
            "title": title,
            "text": text,
            "url": url,
            "image": image,
            "tags": tags
        }
        events.append(event)
    return events

def get_event(i):
    date = datetime.now().strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
    title = f"Event {i}"
    text = f"This is the text for event {i}."
    url = f"https://example.com/event{i}"
    image = f"https://example.com/image{i}.jpg"
